title: Lepechinia fragrans
taxonomy: Lepechinia fragrans is classified in the plant kingdom as a member of the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. It belongs to the genus Lepechinia, which is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas. Lepechinia fragrans is further classified as a species within the genus, characterized by its unique fragrant flowers. The classification of Lepechinia fragrans is also supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and arrangement. At the highest taxonomic level, Lepechinia fragrans belongs to the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Lepechinia fragrans is known for its traditional medicinal uses in various cultures. Its essential oil contains compounds that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Traditionally, it has been used to treat digestive issues and to reduce fever. Additionally, it has been used as a natural remedy for respiratory issues such as colds and bronchitis. Further research is needed to fully understand its medicinal potential and confirm its effectiveness in treating various health conditions.

constituents: Lepechinia fragrans contains sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are known to contribute to its medicinal properties. parts: Lepechinia fragrans, also known as blue skullcap, is a plant with a long history of traditional use in medicine. The medicinal parts of Lepechinia fragrans are primarily the leaves and flowers, which are used to make teas and infusions. These plant parts are believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate various health issues. preparations: Lepechinia fragrans preparations are not well-documented in the available literature, but the plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. side_effects: Lepechinia fragrans is a plant species used in traditional medicine, and while it may have potential health benefits, there is limited information available on its side effects. intro: Lepechinia fragrans, also known as Mexican mint or yerba de la montaña, is a perennial shrub native to the mountainous regions of Mexico and Central America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and inflammation, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Lepechinia fragrans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Lepechinia fragrans is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as in tinctures and essential oils, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including coughs, colds, and digestive issues.
